Title: The strawberry transcription factor FaRAV1 positively regulates anthocyanin accumulation by activation of FaMYB10 and anthocyanin pathway genes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation during fruit ripening; antioxidant/nutritional quality trait
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Ripening fruit receptacle, initiating in apical region
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: RAP expression is positively associated with anthocyanin accumulation and is induced downstream of FaMYB10, but is not directly activated by FaRAV1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated 8.1-fold in FaRAV1-overexpressing fruit and down-regulated to 0.57-fold in FaRAV1-RNAi fruit
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening; transient FaRAV1 overexpression or RNAi in attached green fruit
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis; dual-luciferase assay showing RAP promoter cannot be activated by FaRAV1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for improving anthocyanin sequestration/transport together with upstream regulators such as FaRAV1/FaMYB10
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a differentially expressed anthocyanin gene; the paper states RAP encodes the principal transporter of anthocyanins and likely operates downstream of FaMYB10 rather than as a direct FaRAV1 target
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAP transcript increased in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in FaRAV1 RNAi fruit. 2. FaRAV1 did not activate the RAP promoter directly. 3. RAP likely functions downstream of FaMYB10 in anthocyanin transport/sequestration.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FaMYB10 (indirect regulatory relationship inferred, not direct physical interaction)

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Anthocyanin biosynthesis / flavonoid biosynthesis / phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies upstream precursor pool for anthocyanin production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key structural gene; not explicitly established here as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Ripening fruit receptacle, especially red regions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 6.7-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in FaRAV1 RNAi fruit; promoter directly activated and bound by FaRAV1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anthocyanin-related expression upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CHS promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural target for stacking with upstream regulators to enhance anthocyanin flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS transcript is strongly induced by FaRAV1 overexpression. 2. FaRAV1 activates the CHS promoter and directly binds it. 3. CHS contributes to increased anthocyanin biosynthetic flux in FaRAV1 OE fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of the direct structural targets of FaRAV1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early flavonoid intermediate leading to anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key structural gene; not established as direct control point here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 5.0-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in FaRAV1 RNAi fruit; promoter activated by FaRAV1 but not directly bound in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CHI promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive biosynthetic target in gene stacking for anthocyanin improvement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI is upregulated by FaRAV1 overexpression and downregulated by FaRAV1 RNAi. 2. FaRAV1 activates the CHI promoter in luciferase assays. 3. Y1H did not support direct FaRAV1 binding, implying indirect activation, likely via FaMYB10 or other TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a direct DNA-binding target of FaRAV1 in Y1H despite promoter activation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to dihydroflavonols
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key structural gene; not explicitly designated rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 4.8-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in RNAi fruit; promoter directly activated and bound by FaRAV1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native F3H promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good structural target to reinforce anthocyanin flux with upstream regulatory engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H is induced by FaRAV1 overexpression and reduced by FaRAV1 silencing. 2. FaRAV1 activates the F3H promoter. 3. FaRAV1 directly binds the F3H promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces immediate late-pathway precursors of anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Strong control point candidate; FaRAV1 showed highest activation on DFR promoter among tested structural genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 2.4-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in RNAi fruit; promoter most strongly activated (3.6-fold) and directly bound by FaRAV1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native DFR promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority structural target because FaRAV1 most strongly activates its promoter; promising for anthocyanin enhancement stacks
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR expression is increased by FaRAV1 overexpression and reduced by FaRAV1 RNAi. 2. Among tested pathway genes, DFR promoter showed the strongest FaRAV1 activation. 3. FaRAV1 directly binds the DFR promoter, supporting a major role in boosting anthocyanin flux.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activation by FaRAV1 was highest among tested structural genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms anthocyanidin precursors of anthocyanin glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key structural gene; not established as direct control point here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 4.5-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in RNAi fruit; promoter activated by FaRAV1 but not directly bound in Y1H
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ANS promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supportive biosynthetic target in multigene engineering
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS transcript responds positively to FaRAV1 overexpression and negatively to FaRAV1 RNAi. 2. FaRAV1 activates the ANS promoter in luciferase assays. 3. No direct promoter binding by FaRAV1 was detected in Y1H, indicating indirect activation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Likely indirectly regulated, potentially via FaMYB10 or other TFs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates anthocyanidins to stable anthocyanin glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms stable anthocyanin end products
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for stable anthocyanin formation; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression increased 3.3-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and decreased in RNAi fruit; promoter directly activated and bound by FaRAV1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in FaRAV1 OE fruit during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GT1 promoter in dual-luciferase assay; FaRAV1 driven by 35S in OE construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important terminal-step engineering target for stable anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GT1 is induced by FaRAV1 overexpression and reduced by FaRAV1 RNAi. 2. FaRAV1 activates the GT1 promoter. 3. FaRAV1 directly binds the GT1 promoter, supporting terminal-step enhancement of anthocyanin formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1 OE fruit vs control; accession NA

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RAV family transcription factor with AP2 DNA-binding domain and B3 domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit pigmentation during ripening; antioxidant/nutritional quality trait
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit receptacle, with accumulation initiating in apical tissue during ripening
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via activation of regulatory and structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream activator with dual-level control; directly activates FaMYB10 and several anthocyanin pathway genes, making it a strong inducer of strawberry anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transient overexpression increases FaMYB10 expression and anthocyanin content; RNAi silencing decreases FaMYB10 expression and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: FaMYB10; CHS; F3H; DFR; GT1; CHI; ANS
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FaMYB10 promoter directly; directly activates CHS, F3H, DFR and GT1 promoters; activates CHI and ANS promoters without direct binding detected
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Fruit ripening; ABA responsiveness; promoter contains ABA-, MeJA-, and light-responsive cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward-like hierarchical regulation: activates FaMYB10 and also directly activates structural genes that are also regulated by FaMYB10
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction with FaMYB10 detected by yeast two-hybrid; does not form a protein complex with FaMYB10 according to this study
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FaMYB10 promoter; CHS promoter; F3H promoter; DFR promoter; GT1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript increases during colour change and fruit ripening in apical, middle and basal sections
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increasing expression through ripening; induced by 50 µM ABA for 6 h and 100 µM ABA for 1 or 6 h in fruit discs
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression in strawberry fruit; pGreen II 0029 62-SK effector in luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development/ripening stages; ABA treatment; transient overexpression and RNAi in attached green fruit
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase screening; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; transient overexpression; transient RNAi; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering/red-breeding of high-anthocyanin strawberry fruit; may be combined with FaMYB10 or structural genes such as DFR/GT1; expression tuning rather than constitutive overexpression may reduce pleiotropic risks because RAV family members are involved in development and stress responses
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects are possible because RAV family TFs are implicated broadly in growth, development, senescence and stress responses, but specific tradeoffs were not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of FaRAV1-overexpressing fruit vs control using cultivated strawberry genome reference; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaRAV1 showed the strongest activation of the FaMYB10 promoter among fruit-expressed FaAP2/ERFs. 2. FaRAV1 directly binds a CAACA motif in the FaMYB10 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. FaRAV1 overexpression increases anthocyanin content 3.6-fold and induces FaMYB10 and structural genes, while RNAi reduces anthocyanin accumulation. 4. FaRAV1 also directly activates CHS, F3H, DFR and GT1 promoters, revealing dual-level control of anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FaRAV1 had the strongest FaMYB10 promoter activation (~4-fold) among screened fruit-expressed AP2/ERFs; DFR promoter was the strongest structural-gene target (3.6-fold activation)

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit pigmentation during ripening; antioxidant/nutritional quality trait
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit receptacle, especially red ripening tissue
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in strawberry and direct target of FaRAV1 in this study
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression is induced 8.1-fold in FaRAV1 OE fruit and reduced in FaRAV1 RNAi fruit, correlating with anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin pathway genes including CHS; CHI; F3H; DFR; ANS; GT1; RAP (as discussed in paper)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive transcriptional activation of anthocyanin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in hierarchical feedforward regulation downstream of FaRAV1
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No protein interaction with FaRAV1 detected in yeast two-hybrid; known in general to function in MBW-related regulation, but specific complex testing was not the focus here
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FaRAV1 promoter-binding regulator upstream; FaRAV1 does not physically interact in Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern precedes anthocyanin accumulation during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-associated expression preceding pigment accumulation; elevated in FaRAV1 OE fruit and reduced in FaRAV1 RNAi fruit
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native FaMYB10 promoter used in dual-luciferase, Y1H and EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase promoter assay; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA of FaRAV1 binding to FaMYB10 promoter; RT-qPCR after FaRAV1 OE/RNAi
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central target for anthocyanin biofortification; can be engineered alone or with FaRAV1 for stronger and possibly more coordinated activation of structural genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context in FaRAV1 OE fruit; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FaMYB10 is a direct transcriptional target of FaRAV1. 2. Its expression precedes anthocyanin accumulation during ripening. 3. FaRAV1-induced anthocyanin accumulation is associated with strong induction of FaMYB10, placing FaMYB10 in a central downstream regulatory position.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because the Results directly center on FaRAV1 activation of the FaMYB10 promoter and consequent anthocyanin regulation
